spec.reasoning: Three safe anchor types are used. Lexical: renamed columns and sheet — 'Source' to 'Origin', 'Co Code' to 'Company Code', 'MAPPED' to 'MAPPING', 'CORE VALUE' to 'BASE VALUE', 'EXPECTED RESULT' to 'OUTPUT RESULT', and 'Sheet1' to 'DataSheet'. Note that 'MAPPED' and 'EXPECTED RESULT' are referenced in the instruction as column D and column F respectively (by letter, not by name), so renaming them is safe and tests whether the model hardcodes header names. The instruction references column D and column F by letter, and the keywords like 'Core Activation' etc. are data-level keywords, not column names, so no column name conflicts with instruction body text. Positional_title_row: inserted a title row on DataSheet, pushing the header from row 1 to row 2 and data from rows 2-71 to rows 3-72. This tests whether the model assumes row 1 is the header. The risk flag 'instruction_references_absolute_rows' is noted — the instruction says 'column D' and 'column F' but does not reference specific row numbers; the answer position F2:F71 will shift to F3:F72 after the title row insert, which the evaluation framework handles. Distractor column: added 'BILLING FLAG' after the last column, which is plausibly similar to the output concept ('Billing PO') but clearly different, testing whether the model writes to the wrong column.
